source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1924 photograph)
title: Seal hunters in canoe at Glacier Bay
description: 1899 Curtis photograph from the Harriman Alaska Expedition showing Tlingit seal hunters paddling a canoe at Glacier Bay. The Indigenous peoples of Southeast Alaska, including the Tlingit, knew these waters and their ice long before the Harriman Expedition named the College Fjord glaciers after East Coast colleges. Cropped to remove the LoC archival scan border.
alt: Historical 1899 photograph of a Tlingit seal hunter paddling a small canoe with a child seated behind, at Glacier Bay, Alaska, with snow-capped mountains in the distance
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-10/11 image sourcing workflow; downloaded and cropped from tile.loc.gov
